description: Originally heard giving diagnostic upwardly slurred "peeeurr" call repeatedly. Followed sound to riparian strip along canal where it was flycatching and perching in some bare branches. Backlighting made appreciation of field marks extremely difficult. The silhouette showed a small flycatcher with long wing extension consistent with a pewee. A brief view of the underparts revealed a lemon wash on the whitish belly, and a whitish throat contrasting weakly with a slightly darker breast. Moderately sized, boat-shaped bill.
